signals.reasoning: BTC is liquid and stable, but the active veto argues against opening fresh exposure and the board still does not show a strong enough edge to override that.
ETH has mildly constructive price action, but funding is still positive and the board remains low-edge. Not enough asymmetry to act on under veto conditions.
SOL has slightly better funding support than ETH, but the board remains no-trade and the live veto still keeps this below trade threshold.
TAO is the cleaner bearish lean among the larger names here: still down hard on the day, now with positive funding, and the mechanical board reads bearish.
WLFI has some reversal ingredients with negative funding and crowded positioning, but the board is still mixed and not strong enough to justify fresh exposure under veto.

KAITO remains the clearest bearish setup on the sheet with a large negative 24h move, positive funding, and a bearish mechanical profile. It is still thin, so conviction stays moderate rather than aggressive.
ARB has decent liquidity and decent strength, but the board remains neutral and funding is positive. Interesting, not actionable.
MINA still has a real squeeze-continuation profile with strong momentum and deeply negative funding, but the spread is ugly and the active veto is the wrong environment to chase it.
VVV is one of the more interesting continuation longs with good momentum, better liquidity, and supportive funding, but the live veto still keeps it in watch-only territory.
TNSR still screens as a strong squeeze-continuation long mechanically, but the active veto is a direct reason not to open fresh high-beta exposure. Strong watchlist long, not an active trade.
CHILLGUY is moving hard, but the market is too thin, too wide, and too funding-rich on the long side to trust as a fresh trade in a veto regime.
